prompt:
A clothing store purchased 100 identical t-shirts for $15 each. They decided to price them differently: 
- 60% of the t-shirts were marked up by 50% of their cost price.
- The remaining 40% of the t-shirts were marked up by 25% of their cost price.

During a special promotion, the store sold 75% of the *total* t-shirts at a 20% discount from their respective marked-up prices. The remaining t-shirts were later sold at a 10% discount from their *original* respective marked-up prices (before any promotion discounts were applied).

Assuming sales from both categories (60% and 40% markup) were proportional to their initial quantities during both sales events, what was the total profit made by the store?
